content.claim: Hungary shows high socioeconomic correlation with performance (23.1% impact of SES background) versus OECD average (14.8%), combined with lower share of top performers (9.3% vs 12.6% OECD average) and higher share of low performers (28.1% vs 23.0% OECD average).
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: Describes outcome inequality without isolating tracking mechanism from other policy factors
Does not control for baseline ability composition or prior achievement differences
SES measured as composite variable that may mask specific disadvantage pathways
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
